meals.name: Healthy Chicken Pot Pie
ings: 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp black pepper
1 cup unsweetened almond milk (or low-fat milk)
3 cups cooked shredded chicken (rotisserie works great)
½ cup frozen pearl onions (or ½ small onion, diced and sautéed)
1 tsp fresh thyme (or ½ tsp dried)
1 store-bought deep dish pie crust (Great Value works — use as top crust only)
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Lightly grease a 9-inch pie dish or use a cast iron skillet.

Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or deep skillet over medium-high. Add mushrooms and cook 8 min until browned and sweated. Add carrots, celery, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook 3 more min until carrots begin to soften.
Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir well. Cook 2 minutes so the flour loses its raw taste.
Pour in chicken broth slowly, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Add almond milk. Cook over medium heat, stirring regularly, until sauce thickens — about 5–6 minutes. Do not boil hard.
Stir in shredded chicken, frozen peas, pearl onions, and thyme. Taste and adjust salt. Remove from heat.
Transfer filling to your prepared pie dish (or leave in cast iron if oven-safe).
Unroll the pie crust from the package. Lay it over the top of the dish, trimming to leave about ½ inch overhang. Press edges to seal and crimp with a fork. Cut 3–4 small slits to vent steam.
Brush crust all over with beaten egg wash.
Bake 20–25 minutes until crust is deeply golden and filling is bubbling at the edges. Let rest 5–10 minutes before serving — the filling will thicken as it sits.
meals.note: 💡 Top-crust only keeps this lighter and the crust stays flaky (no soggy bottom). Rotisserie chicken is the easy shortcut here. The Great Value deep dish crust works perfectly as a top-only crust — just unroll it over the dish.
meals.label: Meal A

meals.name: Marry Me Chicken Gnocchi
ings: 1¼ lb boneless skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
2 Tbsp olive oil (or oil from the sun-dried tomato jar)
1 small shallot or ½ onion, diced
⅔ cup sun-dried tomatoes (oil-packed), drained and chopped
1 cup half-and-half (or ¾ cup heavy cream)
Red pepper flakes (optional, for adults only)
steps: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Heat 1 Tbsp oil in a large high-sided skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken in a single layer 3–4 min per side until golden (doesn't need to be cooked through). Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and shallot to the skillet; sauté 2–3 min. Add garlic and sun-dried tomatoes; cook 1 min until fragrant.
Add uncooked gnocchi, breaking apart any stuck pieces. Pour in chicken broth and half-and-half; season with salt and pepper. Cover and cook over medium 5 min until gnocchi are tender and fluffy.
Return chicken to the pan. Add spinach; cook uncovered 3–5 min until chicken is cooked through and spinach is wilted.
Stir in Parmesan until melted and the sauce is creamy. Top with torn basil. Serve with red pepper flakes on the side for adults.
meals.note: 💡 The base recipe is mild — keep the red pepper flakes off to the side so the kids can eat it. Gnocchi cooks right in the sauce, no boiling needed.
meals.label: Meal B

meals.name: Teriyaki Chicken Tacos with Sesame Cucumber Slaw
ings: ⅓ cup + ¼ cup teriyaki sauce
1 avocado sliced, ¼ cup crushed peanuts, sesame seeds
Slaw: 1 large English cucumber, thinly sliced
¼ cup Greek yogurt, 1½ Tbsp mayo
2 Tbsp rice vinegar, 1 Tbsp sweet chili sauce
1 tsp sesame oil, fresh cilantro and green onions
steps: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Add to Instant Pot with ⅓ cup teriyaki sauce. Pressure cook 10 min.
Thinly slice cucumbers. Whisk dressing ingredients. Toss with cucumbers and herbs.
Release Instant Pot. Shred chicken. Spread on lined baking sheet, drizzle with remaining teriyaki and sweet chili sauce. Broil 3–6 min until caramelized.
Warm tortillas. Layer with chicken, cucumber slaw, avocado, crushed peanuts, sesame seeds.
meals.note: 💡 Crockpot: high 2.5–3.5 hrs or low 4–5 hrs. Oven: 400°F for 35 min. Always broil at the end for caramelized edges.
meals.label: Meal C
